channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Computer Science Thesis Defense
item.description: Date: Friday, March 13, 2026 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM - PhD Candidate: Yue Gruszecki Advisor: Prof. Elliot Anshelevich Date and Time: Friday, March 13th, 2026, 9:00 am Location: CII 3130 Webex: https://rensselaer.webex.com/meet/hany4 Thesis Title: Facility Location Problems: Multiple Objectives and Strategic Clients
item.author: Students

item.title: Personal Statement Workshop for Law School Applicants
item.description: Students who are currently in Pre-Law and those interested in the program, are invited to attend the Personal Statement Workshop for Law School Applicants on March 11, from 4 – 5:30 at Sage Labs, room 4304. There, you will get to work on your personal statement with support from the HASS Hub and Student Success Writing Lab.
item.author: Students

item.title: Graduate Student Lunch and Learn-Easy Food Prep with SODEXO- Seating is limited. Students must register.
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 12:00 PM - 2:30 PM - Join us for Easy Food Prep with SODEXO-Lunch will be served after. Seating is limited. No walk-ins. Students must register. Email Noelle Sanchez at sanchn2@rpi.edu to RSVP.
item.author: Students

item.title: Call for Abstracts - Graduate Research Symposium
item.description: Date: Friday, March 13, 2026 (all day) - All graduate students are encouraged to submit abstracts for the 11th Annual Graduate Research Symposium, scheduled for Friday, April 3. The deadline to submit abstracts is Friday, March 13.
item.author: Students

item.title: Competitions for Graduate School Salary and Tuition Info Session March 18th 10am
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM - Competitive fellowships provide a salary and tuition while you earn a graduate degree. Graduate students, seniors, and juniors: come learn how to find and apply to programs that will fund fall 2027 and beyond. Programs are available at all stages of your academic career. International students are welcome! Register for the virtual session. https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_6L3FFwv0qCROrvE Email madige@rpi.edu for more information.
item.author: Students

item.title: School of Architecture Co-terminal Information Session
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 12:30 PM - 1:30 PM - Are you interested in opportunities to pursue graduate studies? Join us for a slice of pizza and a chance to hear about the different master’s degrees that the School of Architecture offers at one of the upcoming information sessions. We’ll talk about the benefits of co-terminal programs and the professional benefits of graduating with a master’s degree. Interested students are encouraged to attend. The co-terminal application deadline is…
item.author: Students

item.title: Transfer Student Coffee Hour
item.description: Date: Friday, March 13, 2026 8:30 AM - 10:00 AM - The Transfer Coffee Hour is a social event that give transfer students a chance to meet one another and some of our staff and faculty. We will have a variety of coffee, tea and hot chocolate available and some tasty breakfast treats as well.
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Summer Student Employment
item.description: The RPI Pipeline Office is currently accepting student applications to work in part-time and full-time positions on campus, this July, with the 2026 RPI STEP and the RPI PREFACE programs, mentoring pre-college students. Applications will be accepted until all positions are filled.
item.author: Students

item.title: Jofa’s Birthday Paw-ty
item.description: Date: Monday, March 16, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Join Presidential Pup Jofa to celebrate her 5th Birthday. Free Cupcakes. Stop by to pet or just say Happy Birthday!! Bring along any donation of pet food/pet product and receive free gift. All items collected will be donated for local animal needs.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Semiconductor CoLab Catalyst Seminar Series – March 2026 (Registration Required)
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 2:30 PM - 4:30 PM - The RPI Semiconductor CoLab Catalyst Seminar Series brings together faculty, researchers, and industry partners to share ideas, highlight collaborations, and advance semiconductor and microelectronics innovation. The seminar on March 17th, at the Albany NanoTech Complex, features Jacob Merson (RPI) and Dureseti (Chidu) Chidambarrao (IBM) in a two 45-min talks with a networking break. Registration is required. Follow the link for more information.
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics and Astronomy Colloquium: Dr. Julian Georg, RPI
item.description: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Title: The Cosmic Microwave Background Explained for Everyone Abstract: The Universe is permeated by photons that are cosmological in origin and almost as old as the Universe itself. They are what we call the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB). In this talk, I will describe how we observe the CMB and what information we can extract from it, as well as explain the physics behind it. This talk is explicitly aimed undergraduate students regardless of…
item.author: Community

item.title: Guest Speaker Deren Pulley: Is the Gut Holding You?
item.description: Deren Pulley’s dissertation explores how people persist when the gut itself – the mediator of subjective thresholds – is lost in order to live otherwise. Forever grieving the gut through techniques of the body, new forms of excremental intimacy, and mourning practices offers an ethical possibility, beyond repair, for living in the ruins of gut collapse. Join on March 18 at 11:30am, in Sage 5711.
item.author: Community

item.title: Get Ready for your Exams in the Success Center
item.description: Join us March 9-12 to prepare for upcoming exams this week! Drop-in Sessions: Calculus II - 7-9pm Essence of Engineering Mechanics - 7-9pm Multivariable Calculus - 7-9pm IEA - 7-9pm Data Structures - 8-10pm
item.author: Community

item.title: Success Center Tutors Hosting Review Sessions
item.description: Cal II: Tuesday, 3/10 7-9pm in the Nason Classroom ENGR Dynamics: Thursday, 3/12 7-9pm at the Success Center
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday March 13: Webex Event Training
item.description: Hosting a campus event or supporting a student webinar? Join a hands-on Webex Event Training Webinar, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. Register now for this webinar presented via Webex on Friday March 13 from 11am-12pm: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700 Learn the basics of Webex Event hosting, with demonstration and Q&A, along with new features recently added to the hosting platform.
item.author: Community

item.title: Concert on March 11 - CODE POEMS: Migrations and Angels
item.description: The electroacoustic duo CODE POEMS, featuring Matt Sargent (composition, guitar, electronics) and Taylor Long (percussion), is dedicated to performing experimental works by living composers. The program features two pieces written expressly for the ensemble: Matthew Goodheart’s “Migrations,” for percussion and lap steel with multichannel electronics, and Matt Sargent’s “Several Angels,” for percussion and electric guitar with real-time scoring. Join at RPI Chapel and Cultural Center from 7-8:30.
item.author: Community

item.title: MANE Special Seminar with Dr. Deyu Li
item.description: Date: Friday, March 13, 2026 1:00 AM - 2:00 AM - “Nanoscale thermal transport – beyond the classical size effect”, Dr. Deyu Li, Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering at Vanderbilt University
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff Quick Learn Webinar - Creating Basic Pivot Tables with Excel for Windows
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 12, 2026 11:00 AM - 11:30 AM - Do you work with data in Excel? Are you always manipulating the content with filters and sorting? If so, taking a look at Pivot Tables in Excel could be beneficial. This workshops will focus on using Pivot Tables in Excel to summarize, filter and group data. Basic functions will be applied as well as a quick look at charts. Please register using the Event URL in the See section of the calendar event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: RPI at Work MBA for Professionals Review
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 12:00 PM - 12:30 PM - Learn to lead from every aspect of an organization with a real-world MBA experience. Prepare to lead the most dynamic and innovative organizations. Engage, involve, motivate, and lead others to reach their fullest potential. Join RPI Dean Aric Krause, Ph. D., as he reviews this innovative project-based, online MBA for professionals program. Please register to receive login details to join this virtual event.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Faculty & Staff Commencement Information
item.description:
The March 20th deadline for regalia is quickly approaching! Please visit the Faculty & Staff Information page to confirm your attendance and place your regalia order. All members participating as a member of a stage party for commencement or hooding for Ph.D. are required to wear regalia.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
